Banking.rate_hike_impact: negative short-term — cost of funds rises, margins compress on variable-rate loans
Banking.rate_cut_impact: positive — stimulates credit demand, improves loan affordability
Banking.dirham_weakness_impact: mixed — pan-African banks (ATW, BOA) lose on currency translation; domestic-only banks neutral
Banking.gdp_growth_impact: positive — economic expansion drives loan demand and reduces defaults
Banking.africa_instability_impact: negative for ATW/BOA — political crises in Côte d'Ivoire, Senegal, or Cameroon directly hit their African subsidiary earnings
Banking.remittance_impact: positive — Moroccan diaspora remittances (~$10bn/yr) support household loan repayment and deposit base

Mining / Fertilizers.commodity_rise_impact: strongly positive — direct revenue uplift for respective metals
Mining / Fertilizers.oil_rise_impact: negative — energy-intensive operations across all mines
Mining / Fertilizers.global_recession_impact: negative — industrial and agricultural commodity demand falls
Mining / Fertilizers.war_conflict_impact: positive for OCP — conflicts in grain-producing regions (Ukraine, Middle East) raise food insecurity, driving fertilizer demand as importing nations rush to secure harvests; also raises phosphate spot prices
Mining / Fertilizers.silver_note: MNG (Managem) is Morocco's largest silver producer via the Imiter mine — silver price moves directly impact MNG revenue even when gold is flat
Mining / Fertilizers.china_demand_note: MNG's cobalt and copper are sold largely to Chinese EV battery and electronics manufacturers; a Chinese industrial slowdown or EV subsidy cut directly hits MNG volumes
Mining / Fertilizers.dap_map_note: OCP's key products are DAP and MAP fertilizers, not raw phosphate rock — track DAP spot price (CFR Brazil/India) as the most direct revenue signal, not generic 'phosphate price'
Mining / Fertilizers.food_security_note: OCP benefits when global grain stocks fall or drought hits major producing nations — farmers increase fertilizer application to maximize yield per acre

Telecommunications.rate_hike_impact: low — telecom is defensive with stable cash flows
Telecommunications.gdp_growth_impact: low — defensive stock, not strongly correlated to economic cycle
Telecommunications.africa_expansion_note: IAM's subsidiary Moov Africa operates in 12 sub-Saharan countries — subscriber growth there is a separate earnings driver from Moroccan operations
Telecommunications.note: Most defensive sector on the BVC; moves primarily on company-specific news and dividends

Cement.rate_hike_impact: negative — slows mortgage origination and housing starts
Cement.oil_rise_impact: negative — kiln fuel costs (petcoke and fuel oil) rise directly
Cement.gdp_growth_impact: positive — construction closely tracks economic growth
Cement.government_spend_impact: strongly positive — infrastructure contracts are bulk cement demand
Cement.world_cup_note: Morocco co-hosting the 2030 World Cup (with Spain/Portugal) requires ~$3-4bn of stadium, road, rail, and airport construction — multi-year cement demand tailwind through 2029
Cement.strait_hormuz_note: A Strait of Hormuz closure raises petcoke and fuel oil import costs for kilns, squeezing cement margins — especially for LHM which is more energy-intensive

Energy / Fuel Distribution.oil_rise_impact: mixed — higher pump prices raise revenue but compress margins if caps prevent full pass-through
Energy / Fuel Distribution.dirham_weakness_impact: negative — imported refined products cost more in MAD
Energy / Fuel Distribution.strait_hormuz_note: Morocco imports ~90% of its refined fuel needs via sea. A Strait of Hormuz closure or Red Sea disruption causes supply tightness and price spikes. TQM's margin depends on how fast the government adjusts or lifts price caps in response
Energy / Fuel Distribution.opec_cut_impact: negative — supply cuts raise crude benchmark, increasing TQM's input costs before any retail price adjustment
Energy / Fuel Distribution.geopolitical_note: Middle East wars that threaten Gulf oil supply routes are the single biggest tail risk for TQM — monitor Iran/Gulf tensions and Houthi Red Sea attacks as leading indicators

Real Estate.rate_hike_impact: strongly negative — mortgage affordability falls, buyer demand collapses
Real Estate.rate_cut_impact: strongly positive — unlocks latent housing demand
Real Estate.remittance_impact: positive — diaspora buyers (France, Spain, Italy, Gulf) represent a significant share of ADH/ADI VEFA pre-sales; EUR and Gulf currency strength vs MAD boosts their purchasing power
Real Estate.government_program_note: ADH is heavily exposed to social housing quotas and state subsidies — watch for budget law changes that affect housing subsidy envelopes

Retail / Consumer.inflation_impact: mixed — boosts headline revenue but squeezes margins on non-food items
Retail / Consumer.gdp_growth_impact: positive — higher incomes drive discretionary spending
Retail / Consumer.war_supply_chain_note: Wars disrupting global shipping (Red Sea, Black Sea) raise import costs for consumer goods — LBV sources significant inventory internationally

Insurance.rate_rise_impact: positive — higher bond yields boost investment income on the float
Insurance.gdp_growth_impact: positive — higher incomes drive insurance adoption
Insurance.catastrophe_note: The 2023 Al Hoceima earthquake was a reminder of Morocco's seismic risk — a major event raises claims sharply. Also monitor flood seasons in the Souss-Massa region

Ports / Logistics.dirham_weakness_impact: positive — some fees are USD/EUR denominated
Ports / Logistics.red_sea_note: Red Sea / Houthi attack disruptions reroute container ships around the Cape of Good Hope, bypassing Mediterranean ports — this reduces MSA's transit volumes but may boost Atlantic-route calls at Casablanca port
Ports / Logistics.ocp_link_note: MSA's Jorf Lasfar terminal handles a large share of OCP's phosphate and fertilizer exports — OCP production growth directly drives MSA throughput growth
Ports / Logistics.world_cup_note: World Cup 2030 logistics and construction material imports will temporarily spike port volumes through Morocco's terminals

Hospitality.eur_mad_rise_impact: positive — European tourists spend more in MAD terms
Hospitality.global_recession_impact: negative — discretionary travel is first cut
Hospitality.world_cup_note: RISMA (Accor-branded hotels: Novotel, Mercure, Sofitel) is the primary listed beneficiary of World Cup 2030. Hotel occupancy and ADR (average daily rate) in Casablanca, Rabat, and Marrakech will surge during the tournament and benefit from infrastructure spending in the years prior
Hospitality.mena_stability_note: Regional conflicts (Libya, Sahel, Israel-Gaza) affect European travel confidence to North Africa broadly — even if Morocco is stable, perception risk reduces bookings
Hospitality.airfare_note: High oil prices raise European airfares, reducing tourist budget and arrivals — especially from low-cost carrier (Ryanair, easyJet) routes that are price-sensitive

sensitive_to: Middle East and Africa fintech growth
Fintech / Payments.dirham_weakness_impact: positive — significant international revenues in USD/EUR
Fintech / Payments.geopolitical_note: HPS sells payment software to banks and processors in the Gulf, Africa, and Europe — Gulf bank technology spending is influenced by oil revenues; a sustained low oil price can delay Gulf client contract renewals
Fintech / Payments.note: Low-liquidity stock; large price moves on thin volume are common

Food / Agribusiness.sugar_price_rise_impact: negative — raises input costs if government caps prevent price pass-through
Food / Agribusiness.el_nino_note: El Niño typically reduces Brazil sugar cane yields (world's largest producer), pushing global sugar prices higher — negative for CSUMAR as an importer/refiner under state price controls
Food / Agribusiness.drought_note: Poor beet harvests in the Doukkala region (CSUMAR's domestic sugar beet zone) force higher imports at world prices — margin squeeze if retail prices are capped
Food / Agribusiness.war_supply_note: Black Sea conflicts (Ukraine war) disrupted global sugar supply chains and raised freight costs — any re-escalation would lift CSUMAR's import costs

Steel.scrap_price_rise_impact: negative — main raw material for Sonasid's electric arc furnace
Steel.electricity_cost_impact: high — electric arc furnace is extremely electricity-intensive; ONE (Office National de l'Electricité) tariff decisions directly set Sonasid's cost structure
Steel.china_dumping_note: Chinese steel overcapacity leads to subsidized exports at below-market prices — when China floods global markets with cheap steel, it undercuts Sonasid's pricing in the Moroccan and regional market
Steel.war_note: Conflicts that destroy infrastructure create post-war reconstruction demand for rebar — historically positive for regional steel producers once the acute phase ends; ongoing wars raise scrap supply from destroyed equipment
Steel.cbam_note: EU carbon border adjustment could eventually raise costs for Moroccan steel exports to Europe if Morocco's carbon pricing does not align with EU standards
